title: An Overview of readODS
text: can append another sheet existing ods file sheet name “mtcars_ods”. Read specific sheet. Notice row names missing. can also integer sheet, e.g. 2 second sheet. Update existing sheet preserve row names Notice information sheet mtcars_ods updated. Read specific range append existing sheet. update missing sheet.

title: Read and Write ODS Files

code: library(readODS) read_ods("starwars.ods") #>                  Name homeworld species #> 1      Luke Skywalker  Tatooine   Human #> 2               C-3PO  Tatooine   Human #> 3               R2-D2  Alderaan   Human #> 4         Darth Vader  Tatooine   Human #> 5         Leia Organa  Tatooine   Human #> 6           Owen Lars  Tatooine   Human #> 7  Beru Whitesun lars   Stewjon   Human #> 8               R5-D4  Tatooine   Human #> 9   Biggs Darklighter  Kashyyyk Wookiee #> 10     Obi-Wan Kenobi  Corellia   Human read_ods("starwars.ods", sheet = 2) #>                  Name height mass    hair_color skin_color eye_color birth_year #> 1      Luke Skywalker    172   77         blond       fair      blue       19.0 #> 2               C-3PO    202  136          none      white    yellow       41.9 #> 3               R2-D2    150   49         brown      light     brown       19.0 #> 4         Darth Vader    178  120   brown, grey      light      blue       52.0 #> 5         Leia Organa    165   75         brown      light      blue       47.0 #> 6           Owen Lars    183   84         black      light     brown       24.0 #> 7  Beru Whitesun lars    182   77 auburn, white       fair blue-gray       57.0 #> 8               R5-D4    188   84         blond       fair      blue       41.9 #> 9   Biggs Darklighter    228  112         brown    unknown      blue      200.0 #> 10     Obi-Wan Kenobi    180   80         brown       fair     brown       29.0 #>    gender #> 1    male #> 2    male #> 3  female #> 4    male #> 5  female #> 6    male #> 7    male #> 8    male #> 9    male #> 10   male read_ods("starwars.ods", sheet = 2, range = "A1:C11") #>                  Name height mass #> 1      Luke Skywalker    172   77 #> 2               C-3PO    202  136 #> 3               R2-D2    150   49 #> 4         Darth Vader    178  120 #> 5         Leia Organa    165   75 #> 6           Owen Lars    183   84 #> 7  Beru Whitesun lars    182   77 #> 8               R5-D4    188   84 #> 9   Biggs Darklighter    228  112 #> 10     Obi-Wan Kenobi    180   80

title: Read and Write ODS Files

what: About the speed and file size
title: Read and Write ODS Files
text: package written entirely R. Although efficiency improved, please don’t expect heavily optimized performance readxl, readr data.table’s fread. Also, package can’t handle ODS files larger “medium size”. See issue #71. need read write large ODS files efficiently, headless interface LibreOffice recommended. See issue #49 example.

title: Read and Write ODS Files
text: logo readODS remix LibreOffice Calc v6.1 icon created Document Foundation. original LibreOffice logo licensed Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike 3.0 Unported License. readODS product Document Foundation. logo readODS licensed Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike 3.0 Unported License. creator package Gerrit-Jan Schutten. current maintainer Chung-hong Chan. package benefits contributions Thomas J. Leeper, John Foster, Sergio Oller, Jim Hester, Stephen Watts, Arthur Katossky, Stas Malavin, Duncan Garmonsway, Mehrad Mahmoudian, Matt Kerlogue. package emulates behaviors readxl::read_xlsx, writexl::write_xlsx xlsx::write.xlsx. package silent member rio, don’t need care file format anymore.

what: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
text: read_ods function read single sheet ods file return data frame. read.ods always returns list data frames one data frame per sheet. wrapper read_ods backward compatibility previous version readODS. Please use read_ods possible.

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
code: read_ods(   path,   sheet = 1,   col_names = TRUE,   col_types = NULL,   na = "",   skip = 0,   formula_as_formula = FALSE,   range = NULL,   row_names = FALSE,   strings_as_factors = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  read.ods(file = NULL, sheet = NULL, formulaAsFormula = FALSE)

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
text: path path ods file. sheet sheet read. Either string (sheet name), integer sheet number. default 1. col_names logical, indicating whether file contains names variables first line. Default TRUE. col_types Either NULL guess spreadsheet refer readr::type_convert() specify cols specification. NA return data frame columns "characters". na Character vector strings use missing values. default read_ods converts blank cells missing data. can also set NULL, empty cells treated NA. skip number lines data file skip beginning read data. parameter larger total number lines ods file, empty data frame returned. formula_as_formula logical, switch display formulas formulas "SUM(A1:A3)" resulting value "3"... "8".. . Default FALSE. range selection rectangle using Excel-like cell range, range = "D12:F15" range = "R1C12:R6C15". Cell range processing handled cellranger package. row_names logical, indicating whether file contains names rows first column. Default FALSE. strings_as_factors logical, character columns converted factors. Default FALSE. verbose logical, messages displayed. Default FALSE. file read.ods , path ods file. formulaAsFormula read.ods , switch display formulas formulas "SUM(A1:A3)" resulting value "3"... "8"..

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
text: data frame (data.frame) containing representation data ods file.

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
text: Currently, ods files linked external data source read. Merged cells parsed correctly.

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
text: Chung-hong Chan chainsawtiney@gmail.com, Gerrit-Jan Schutten phonixor@gmail.com

title: Read Data From ODS File — read_ods
code: if (FALSE) { # Read a file read_ods("starwars.ods") # Read a specific sheet, e.g. the 2nd sheet read_ods("starwars.ods", sheet = 2) # Read a specific range, e.g. A1:C11 read_ods("starwars.ods", sheet = 2, range = "A1:C11") }

what: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
text: Function write single data.frame ods file.

title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
code: write_ods(   x,   path,   sheet = "Sheet1",   append = FALSE,   update = FALSE,   row_names = FALSE,   col_names = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   overwrite = NULL )

title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
text: x data.frame path Path ods file write sheet Name sheet append logical, TRUE indicates x appended existing file (path) new sheet. sheet sheet_name exists, exception thrown. See update. Default FALSE. update logical, TRUE indicates sheet sheet_name existing file (path) updated content x. sheet sheet_name exist, exception thrown. Default FALSE. row_names logical, TRUE indicates row names x included sheet. Default FALSE. col_names logical, TRUE indicates column names x included sheet. Default FALSE. verbose logical, messages displayed. Default FALSE. overwrite logical, deprecated.

title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
text: ODS file written file path location specified user. value path also returned invisibly.

title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
text: Thomas J. Leeper thosjleeper@gmail.com, John Foster john.x.foster@nab.com.au, Chung-hong Chan chainsawtiney@gmail.com

title: Write Data to ODS File — write_ods
code: if (FALSE) { # preserve the row names write_ods(mtcars, "mtcars.ods", row_names = TRUE) # append a sheet to an existing file write_ods(PlantGrowth, "mtcars.ods", append = TRUE, sheet = "plant") }
